questions.text: If two positive integers a and b are written as a = x³y² and b = xy³, where x, y are prime numbers, then HCF (a, b) is:
questions.explanation: HCF is the product of the smallest power of each common prime factor. Here, the common factors are x¹ and y².

questions.text: The decimal expansion of the rational number 33/2²·5 will terminate after how many places of decimals?
questions.explanation: The number of decimal places is determined by the highest power of 2 or 5 in the denominator (2² in this case).

questions.text: Which of the following is an irrational number?
questions.explanation: √2 cannot be expressed as a ratio of two integers; its decimal expansion is non-terminating and non-recurring.

questions.text: The product of a non-zero rational and an irrational number is:
questions.explanation: The product of a non-zero rational number and an irrational number is always irrational.

questions.text: If the HCF of 65 and 117 is expressible in the form 65m – 117, then the value of m is:
